question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: save(ContractResult)] Given that ContractResult.getId() returns consensusTimestamp and isNew() always returns true, can an unprivileged user insert two ContractResult records with the same consensusTimestamp (one legitimate, one forged) to cause a primary key conflict that corrupts the legitimate authorized transaction record?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/ContractResultRepository.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: save(ContractResult)] Can an unprivileged user insert a ContractResult with a gasLimit set to the maximum Long value, exploiting the lack of gas limit validation to make a contract call appear to have been authorized with unlimited gas, bypassing gas-based authorization controls?
